name: Nicki Parrott
abstract: Nicki Parrott (pronounced pa-ROTT) is a jazz vocalist and bass player from Australia.

interviews.title: Nicki Parrott interviewed by Monk Rowe. Utica, New York, November 6, 2010 [interview 1 of 2]
Nicki Parrott interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 2, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Metz Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: This trio has kind of evolved into the Rossano Sportiello Trio. You know Nicki will front it, or sometimes I will front it... I’d love to record with Nicki and Rossano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Metz Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, October 3, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Rossano Sportiello
about_quotes.evidence: Rossano records and performs frequently on the Arbors label with bassist Nicki Parrott and drummer Eddie Metz.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rossano Sportiello interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, October 2, 2015 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Gene Bertoncini
about_quotes.evidence: And Peter Appleyard, and a lady bass player, Nicki Parrott, and Joe Ascione and Bucky Pizzarelli. And it was for label called Arbors Records.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gene Bertoncini interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Adrian Cunningham
about_quotes.evidence: I think I met Nicki maybe a year or so after moving here, and we’ve played together a lot since then at a lot of festivals
about_quotes.doc_title: Adrian Cunningham interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 31, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Hyman
about_quotes.evidence: And that bass player, what’s her name, Parrott, Vicky is her name? ... Nicki Parrott. Wonderful
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Hyman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001 [interview 2 of 2]
